steps.expected: Canonical login issues a JWT with `companyCode`, omits legacy `cid`, and authenticated `/auth/me` succeeds under canonical context.
steps.observed: `AuthControllerIT#login_and_me_flow_succeeds` passed; the issued access token contained `companyCode=ACME`, did not contain `cid`, and `/api/v1/auth/me` returned `200 OK` with `companyCode=ACME`.

steps.action: Inspect `AuthControllerIT#legacyCompanyIdHeader_doesNotEstablishAuthenticatedContext` and `AuthControllerIT#tenantBindingMismatch_returnsControlledAuthErrorContract` from the same Maven run.
steps.expected: Legacy-only `X-Company-Id` does not establish authenticated context, and canonical mismatch fails closed with the controlled auth envelope.
steps.observed: Both tests passed: legacy-only `X-Company-Id` on `/api/v1/auth/me` returned `403` with `success=false`, and canonical mismatch returned the controlled `AUTH_004` / `COMPANY_CONTEXT_MISMATCH` denial.

steps.action: Inspect `CompanyContextFilterControlPlaneBindingTest` in the same Maven run.
steps.expected: Control-plane requests bind via canonical path/company resolution and reject foreign or missing context instead of honoring legacy compatibility context.
steps.observed: All 12 `CompanyContextFilterControlPlaneBindingTest` cases passed, including canonical path-target binding for super-admin control-plane flows and uniform `403` fail-closed responses for foreign, unknown, anonymous, and malformed targets.

evidence.result: BUILD SUCCESS; Tests run: 26, Failures: 0, Errors: 0, Skipped: 0.
evidence.consoleErrors: The scoped Maven run finished with `Tests run: 26, Failures: 0, Errors: 0` and `BUILD SUCCESS`. Negative-path logs showed the expected controlled company-mismatch rejection; newly issued JWTs omitted `cid`.

assertions.title: Public reset bypass stays public, tenant binding stays canonical

steps.action: Inspect `CompanyContextFilterPasswordResetBypassTest` from the scoped Maven run.
steps.expected: Public forgot/reset endpoints remain reachable without tenant binding, while non-public lookalikes or wrong methods are still blocked.
steps.observed: All 5 bypass tests passed: `POST /api/v1/auth/password/forgot`, `POST /api/v1/auth/password/reset`, and the retired alias bypassed company binding with `200`, while `GET /api/v1/auth/password/forgot` and `/forgot/extra` were rejected with `403`.

steps.action: Run `MIGRATION_SET=v2 mvn -Djacoco.skip=true -Dtest=PasswordResetServiceTest#requestResetScopeUsesLegacyCompanyHeaderWhenPrimaryHeaderMissing test` to observe the legacy header variant on the public forgot flow.
steps.expected: Legacy `X-Company-Id` does not create tenant binding for the public forgot flow and is only ignored/observed for global-identity handling.
steps.observed: The targeted test passed and logged `event=password_reset.scope ... tenantContext=LEGACY_TENANT outcome=tenant_context_ignored`, confirming the legacy header variant did not establish tenant binding for forgot-password handling.

steps.action: Reuse `AuthControllerIT#legacyCompanyIdHeader_doesNotEstablishAuthenticatedContext` and `AuthControllerIT#tenantBindingMismatch_returnsControlledAuthErrorContract` from the main Maven run.
steps.expected: Authenticated endpoints continue to require canonical `X-Company-Code` / `companyCode` context and fail closed on legacy-only or mismatched context.
steps.observed: Authenticated `/api/v1/auth/me` stayed canonical-only: legacy-only `X-Company-Id` returned `403`, and mismatched canonical context returned the controlled `AUTH_004` denial with `COMPANY_CONTEXT_MISMATCH`.

evidence.result: Both scoped Maven runs finished with BUILD SUCCESS; the follow-up method-level run confirmed `tenant_context_ignored` for the legacy header variant.
evidence.consoleErrors: No unexpected console errors. The legacy-header follow-up run ended with `Tests run: 1, Failures: 0, Errors: 0` and logged `outcome=tenant_context_ignored`; the main scoped run ended with `BUILD SUCCESS`.

frictions.description: The prompt's suggested Maven selector did not directly exercise the public forgot flow with the legacy `X-Company-Id` header variant.
frictions.resolution: Ran one additional narrow method-level Maven command for `PasswordResetServiceTest#requestResetScopeUsesLegacyCompanyHeaderWhenPrimaryHeaderMissing` and captured a separate evidence log/report.

summary: Tested 2 assigned assertions on the jvm-tests surface: 2 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked. Canonical company binding remained enforced for authenticated routes, legacy `X-Company-Id` / `cid` compatibility did not establish context, newly issued JWTs omitted `cid`, and public forgot/reset flows remained reachable without tenant binding while ignoring legacy header context.
